name: HookCycler
description:
Library component. Multi-cycle variant of Hook -- fires PreCompact up to maxCycles times, gated on observing the Agent back at Active between fires. Used by substrate models (sling-3768) that need to simulate N consecutive context-cycle events.

Distinct from `lib/hook.kinner.json`, which is the original single-fire Hook used by work-coordination composites like Lane / convoy-lands. The two components were split in 2026-04-29 after sling-3768's multi-cycle additions broke single-fire consumers (E009 on the new AGENT_OBSERVE binding plus a behavior change from unconditional fire to agent-gated fire).

Use `Hook` for: any topology where the lifecycle hook fires once per modeled boundary (most cases).
Use `HookCycler` for: substrate models that need N substrate cycles in one TLC run.

The coupling between HookCycler and other actors is intentionally loose: it fires whenever Agent is Active and the counter is below max. It does NOT synchronize with Patrol or any per-cycle scheduler. Real gas-town's PreCompact fires based on context-window pressure (a runtime/time signal), independent of patrol or substrate state. The model preserves that loose coupling so TLC explores realistic interleavings.

AGENT.description: fires the hook event at the bound agent. Tagged 'PreCompact'.

AGENT_OBSERVE.description: observes the bound agent's state. HookCycler only fires PreCompact when agent is Active (the only state where the Active->Compacting receive triple is enabled).

actors.name: HookCycler
actors.owner: Lifecycle event firer that fires PreCompact up to maxCycles times. Single Idle state with a self-loop guarded by cycleCount < maxCycles. cycleCount is a 134-shape typed variable bound to [0, maxCycles]; assign increments it on each fire.

triples.event: lifecycle moment reached: PreCompact fires (loop entry)
